steps.title: Use the angle sum
steps.content:
The angles in a triangle sum to $180^\circ$. Since the measure of angle $U$ is $58^\circ$, the other two angles sum to
$$
180^\circ-58^\circ=122^\circ
$$

steps.title: Limit the possible value
steps.content: Both remaining angles must be positive, so the measure of angle $V$ must be less than $122^\circ$. Of the choices, only $121$ is less than $122$.

steps.title: Check the remaining angle
steps.content: If the measure of angle $V$ is $121^\circ$, then $$ m(W)=122^\circ-121^\circ=1^\circ $$ This is possible. The answer is A.
